programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A 1981 "Raiders of the Lost Ark" prototype; a 1788 Elizabeth Shoemaker sampler; 1976 and 1977 Roy De Forest mixed media artwork.

programme.title: Amanpour and Company
programme.desc: Christiane Amanpour leads discussions about world issues and interviews with global leaders.

programme.title: BBC News The Context
programme.desc: A regular panel of guests from across the political spectrum discuss the biggest international stories of the day.

programme.title: NJ Spotlight News with Briana Vannozzi
programme.desc: Statewide reporting on the latest news and issues affecting communities across the Garden State.

programme.title: DW News
programme.desc: Presenting a round-up of the most recent news and developments from across the world.

programme.title: BBC News America
programme.desc: Live news program produced out of BBC's Washington, D.C. bureau; host Sumi Somaskanda.

programme.title: PBS News Hour
programme.desc: Co-anchors Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett and correspondents offer in-depth analysis of current events.

programme.title: The Making of the American Revolution
programme.desc: A behind the scenes look at "The American Revolution" with Ken Burns, Sarah Botstein and David Schmidt to learn about the production, which took nearly a decade to complete.

programme.title: The M Factor 2: Before the Pause-Perimenopause
programme.desc: Exploring solutions for women experiencing perimenopause, the transition years leading to menopause.

programme.title: Amanpour and Company
programme.desc: Christiane Amanpour leads discussions about world issues and interviews with global leaders.

programme.title: One on One With Steve Adubato
programme.desc: Professionals and innovators share their experiences.

programme.title: Nature
programme.sub-title: Becoming Elephant: The Orphans of Reteti: Graduation Day
programme.desc: Following the journey of orphaned elephants at Reteti, the first community-owned elephant sanctuary in Africa.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: NOVA
programme.desc: Four members of the Artemis II crew embark on a perilous 10-day journey to orbit the moon.

programme.title: Secrets of the Dead
programme.desc: The search for the lost remains of formerly enslaved Black Union soldiers who were murdered after the Civil War.

programme.title: Project Chapel
programme.desc: One of the world's only quantum computers is installed in a century old chapel.

programme.title: Lithium Rising: The Race for Critical Minerals
programme.desc: Exploring the drive to decarbonize and its effects on vulnerable communities on the frontlines of extraction.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.sub-title: The Pea and the Princess; D.W. and Dr. Whosit
programme.desc: Prunella is directing the third-grade's production of "The Princess and the Pea," but nothing seems to go according to her plan; D.W. is determined to watch a new television show after her parents tell her she's too young to watch.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.sub-title: A Town Called Trifle; I Am Oddius
programme.desc: During the town of Trifle's 100-year anniversary party, Ozzie and Onom must stop Tracey Trifler from covering it in trifle; Osric and Omni try to stop a Roman gladiator from causing ancient oddness.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: Chris and Martin search for an obscure rain forest creature.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: The Hunters head to a nearby mountain to learn about snow.

programme.title: Wild Kratts: Our Blue and Green World
programme.desc: While doing their annual Laundry Day, the Kratt brothers disagree on what is better -- blue oceans or green forests.

programme.title: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood
programme.sub-title: Daniel's Obstacle Course; Daniel Plays in a Gentle Way
programme.desc: Margaret gets upset when Daniel is too fast and too loud on the obstacle course; Daniel and his friends learn that sometimes it is important to play slowly and gently.

programme.title: Phoebe & Jay
programme.desc: Phoebe, Jay and Lupe practice their fire safety plan when the fire inspector visits; Phoebe and Jay buy juice from Mr. Aguilar.

programme.title: Sesame Street
programme.desc: Chris, Elmo and Abby help Fido feel proud that he's a frog.

programme.title: Work It Out Wombats!
programme.desc: It's Backwards Day in the Treeborhood; Cece, Carly and Clyde have fun with patterns.

programme.title: Let's Learn
programme.sub-title: Can You Hear the Long "i" in Wide?
programme.desc: Comparing by length and height; reading "Daddy's Arms" and "The Girl Who Never Made Mistakes"; learning rhythms from Ghana; blending and decoding oe and ie vowel teams.

programme.title: Elinor Wonders Why
programme.desc: Elinor and her friends investigate the purpose of smell; Elinor is excited to explore with Camilla Dromedary, but Camilla is more interested in playing dress up.

programme.title: Cyberchase
programme.desc: On his annual trip to visit Eugene, Digit finds out that poor conditions in Mermaidos have forced many of the fish from their home.

programme.title: Molly of Denali
programme.desc: Molly and Vera think about teaching more tourists in Qyah about wildfire prevention; Molly can't wait to hunt for seagull eggs.

programme.title: Baking With Julia
programme.desc: Nick Malgieri prepares zaleti, X cookies, amaretti, pizzelles, classic biscotti and tuiles.

programme.title: Ask This Old House
programme.desc: Tightening a loose stair railing; stripping paint off old door hardware; the uses and purposes of electrical boxes; replacing an old thermostat with a smart thermostat.

programme.title: Shakespeare: Rise of a Genius
programme.desc: A 30-year-old Shakespeare is at the top of his game -- he's got a job at England's best theater company, the Lord Chamberlain's Men and the money is rolling in; a tragedy leads to the creation of "Hamlet.".

programme.title: America's Home Cooking: Flavors of the Fourth
programme.desc: Celebrating picnics and barbecue with Chris Fennimore and the America's Home Cooking team; recipes include horseradish potato salad, baked beans, apple hand pies and more summer favorites.

programme.title: Social Security & You With Mary Beth Franklin
programme.desc: Mary Beth Franklin talks about claiming social security, including at what age one should claim it, and provides insight on how to get the most out of retirement benefits.

programme.desc: Tiger Tail USA in Kent, Wash.

programme.title: Samantha Brown's Places to Love
programme.desc: Samantha Brown explores the emotional heart of travel and the people who change, challenge and strengthen a destination.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Exploring the Franconia region of Germany, including Nuremberg, the Imperial Castle and the Schoner Brunnen fountain; a Franconian vineyard.

programme.desc: Professionals and innovators share their experiences.

programme.title: Consuelo Mack WealthTrack

programme.title: History With David Rubenstein

programme.desc: The renowned political scientist discusses global news and interviews world leaders.

programme.title: Firing Line With Margaret Hoover
programme.desc: Margaret Hoover leads interviews and debates.

programme.title: The Chavis Chronicles
programme.desc: Journalist Lonnae O'Neal uncovers generational truths that reveal how land, identity and resilience shape her family's American journey.

programme.title: New York Now
programme.desc: Journalists discuss New York state politics and government.

programme.title: To the Contrary With Bonnie Erbé
programme.desc: All-female news analysis of issues and policy affecting the U.S.

programme.title: The Open Mind
programme.desc: Behavioral neuroscientist Eduardo Mercado talks about his discovery of whale songs and their social implications.

programme.title: Think Tank With Steve Adubato
programme.desc: Steve Adubato holds four-person round table discussions that explore issues facing society.

programme.title: Aging Backwards 4: The Miracle of Flexibility With Miranda Esmonde-White
programme.desc: Gentle flexibility and range of motion exercises can improve posture and help reverse the signs of aging, improve organ health and increase energy.

programme.desc: Celebrating picnics and barbecue with Chris Fennimore and the America's Home Cooking team; recipes include horseradish potato salad, baked beans, apple hand pies and more summer favorites.
